He asserted that the ethical conduct of those in power permeates everything beneath it So when Morgan talks about a board member needing to show respect to a colleague during a budget dispute He's basically proving Aristotle, right? We have modern data on this the notes reference Edgar shines research at MIT Wait, he's the organizational culture guy. He found that an organization's behavior is shaped by deeply held assumptions That's academic speak for how leaders act when things get real It's easy to have good values when profits are up or when the team is winning by 20 points Anyone can be a double-goal coach when they're winning exactly The board's real values aren't in the strategic plan They're revealed in how they treat each other when they disagree on a budget cut the coach's real values aren't in the pregame speech They're revealed when the team is down by 10 points with two minutes left and the ref is blowing calls That is the moment the culture is set speaking of sports and values the source brings up Tony Dungey and I think he is the perfect Counter-argument to the idea that you have to be a loud aggressive Alpha leader to set a strong tone because we usually think tone at the top means volume at the top absolutely Tony Dungey was the first African-American head coach to win a Super Bowl and if you watch him on the sidelines His style was famously quiet. He didn't scream. He didn't rule through fear. He didn't throw headsets He built his teams on consistency character and accountability. He proved that tone doesn't mean volume. That's the key distinction Tone is not volume. It's values made visible Dungey expected his players to follow his lead and because his lead was so steady they did He didn't just win games. He built a culture that survived even when he left the room. So we've established the mechanism here We have the philosophy the data the sports analogies But looking at the current landscape described in the sources, we seem to be in a bit of a crisis This isn't just theoretical organizations are hurting They are in the nonprofit world the data shows boards are struggling significantly You have members who show up quarterly skim the financials nod through the agenda and feel like they've done their duty Meanwhile executive directors are burning out at record rates because they are managing up Right, they're spending all their energy trying to keep the board happy or engaged Instead of leading the organization and the staff sees this they see the gap between the stated values of the mission And the disengagement of leadership that gap is where cynicism is born and in youth sports It's the exact same gap Leagues print beautiful handbooks about respect and then the first tense game happens the parents go wild and the handbook is ignored It's the wallpaper effect in action Everyone knows the values are fake and that breeds profound cynicism. Okay, so let's get practical here for a minute If you are listening to this whether you are a ceo a board member A soccer coach or just a parent How do you fix the wallpaper? How do you make the culture real? Because Be virtuous like play-doh is a pretty tall order for a Tuesday morning The sources give us a really specific actionable tip. They do and I love how deceptively simple this is It's called the 10 minute rule walk us through it. The advice is arrive 10 minutes early Whether it's a board meeting a shift at work or a soccer practice And in those 10 minutes your only job is to have one genuine Non-agenda conversation just talk to someone that's the fix that sounds almost too easy It sounds too small doesn't it but think about the intent if you're a board member you find a staff member Maybe someone you usually ignore if you're a coach you find a player who had a rough week Yes, how are you or how was school and you actually listen to the answer? Why does that change the culture? It shifts the dynamic from transactional to relational It establishes a human connection before the business begins PCA teaches coaches that the first five minutes of practice set the tone for the whole session If you start with connection the rest follows if you start with chaos or detachment You're fighting uphill for the next hour. It forces the leader to be present. You can't fake that. Yes You can't have a genuine conversation if you're checking your email or thinking about your next meeting It forces you to model the engagement you want to see it signals I am here and I see you it's a small investment just 10 minutes But it builds incredible social capital and notice it doesn't cost any money You don't need a consultant for this. You don't need a budget approval. You just need to show up and be a human being It turns the tone at the top from a concept into an action as we wrap this up Let's circle back to that job description We started with the one with the bullet points about governance and duties the one that completely misses the point We've effectively rewritten the job description for anyone in charge of anything today The sources are uncompromising on this. There is no firewall. No firewall. You cannot separate who you are from how you lead The board's culture is the nonprofit's culture The coach's behavior is the team's identity You can't outsource culture to a committee or a document It's a sobering thought whether you are running a fortune 500 company or just running a car pool You are setting a tone You are creating a microculture in that car or that boardroom and the question isn't are you setting a tone? You are whether you like it or not. The question is Is it the tone you want? Intentionality is the only difference between a toxic culture and a thriving one. Well, this has been a fantastic look at the invisible forces that shape Our organizations I'm definitely going to be thinking about that wallpaper analogy the next time I see a mission statement on a wall It's a hard image to shake once you see it before we go I want to leave everyone with that final provocative thought from our source material We talked about emerson saying what you are speak so loudly. I can't hear what you're saying It brings it all back to behavior. So the question for you to mull over today is this If you stop talking right now What are you still saying to the people around you just by being you? That's the question. Thanks for diving in with us. We'll see you next time
